1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import base64
4	from enum import Enum
5	from io import BytesIO
6	from typing import (
7	    Annotated,
8	    Any,
9	    AsyncGenerator,
10	    Generator,
11	    List,
12	    Literal,
13	    Optional,
14	    Union,
15	)
16	
17	import filetype
18	import requests
19	from typing_extensions import Self
20	
21	from llama_index.core.bridge.pydantic import (
22	    AnyUrl,
23	    BaseModel,
24	    ConfigDict,
25	    Field,
26	    FilePath,
27	    field_serializer,
28	    field_validator,
29	    model_validator,
30	)
31	from llama_index.core.constants import DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOW, DEFAULT_NUM_OUTPUTS
32	
33	
34	class MessageRole(str, Enum):
35	    """Message role."""
36	
37	    SYSTEM = "system"
38	    USER = "user"
39	    ASSISTANT = "assistant"
40	    FUNCTION = "function"
41	    TOOL = "tool"
42	    CHATBOT = "chatbot"
43	    MODEL = "model"
44	
45	
46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
52	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
53	    image: bytes | None = None
54	    path: FilePath | None = None
55	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
56	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
57	    detail: str | None = None
58	
59	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
60	    @classmethod
61	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
62	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
63	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
64	            return url
65	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
66	
67	    @model_validator(mode="after")
68	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
69	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
70	
71	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
72	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
73	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
74	        """
75	        if not self.image:
76	            return self
77	
78	        if not self.image_mimetype:
79	            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
80	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
81	
82	        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
83	
84	        return self
85	
86	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
87	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
88	
89	        Args:
90	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
91	        """
92	        if self.image is not None:
93	            if as_base64:
94	                return BytesIO(self.image)
95	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
96	        elif self.path is not None:
97	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
98	            if as_base64:
99	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
100	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
101	        elif self.url is not None:
102	            # load image from URL
103	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
104	            img_bytes = response.content
105	            if as_base64:
106	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
107	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
108	        else:
109	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
110	
111	
112	ContentBlock = Annotated[
113	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
114	]
115

file.content:
Attributes:
        embeddings: Multi-vector dict representation of this resource for embedding-based search/retrieval
        text: Plain text representation of this resource
        data: Raw binary data of the media content
        mimetype: The MIME type indicating the format/type of the media content
        path: Local filesystem path where the media content can be accessed
        url: URL where the media content can be accessed remotely
    """

    embeddings: dict[EmbeddingKind, list[float]] | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Vector representation of this resource."
    )
    data: bytes | None = Field(
        default=None,
        exclude=True,
        description="base64 binary representation of this resource.",
    )
    text: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Text representation of this resource."
    )
    mimetype: str | None = Field(
        default=None, description="MIME type of this resource."
    )
    path: Path | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Filesystem path of this resource."
    )
    url: AnyUrl | None = Field(default=None, description="URL to reach this resource.")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def guess_mimetype(self) -> Self:
        """Guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing its content but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.data or self.mimetype:
            return self

        try:
            decoded_data = base64.b64decode(self.data)
            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_data)
            self.mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        except Exception as e:
            logging.debug("Data is not base64 encoded, cannot guess mimetype")
        finally:
            return self

    @property
    def hash(self) -> str:
        """Generate a hash to uniquely identify the media resource.

        The hash is generated based on the available content (data, path, text or url).
        Returns an empty string if no content is available.
        """
        bits: list[str] = []
        if self.text is not None:
            bits.append(self.text)
        if self.data is not None:
            # Hash the binary data if available
            bits.append(str(sha256(self.data).hexdigest()))
        if self.path is not None:
            # Hash the file path if provided
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.path).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))
        if self.url is not None:
            # Use the URL string as basis for hash
            bits.append(str(sha256(str(self.url).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()))

        doc_identity = "".join(bits)
        if not doc_identity:
            return ""
        return str(sha256(doc_identity.encode("utf-8", "surrogatepass")).hexdigest())


class Node(BaseNode):
    text_resource: MediaResource | None = Field(
        default=None, description="Text content of the node."
    )
    image_resource: MediaResource | None = Field(

content.text: All 12 tests pass. Let me quickly sanity check the new helper with a manual test including a pre-base64-encoded image input.

python -c "
import base64
from llama_index.core.base.llms.types import ImageBlock, normalize_image_bytes

png_1px = base64.b64decode(
    'iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAQAAAC1HAwCAAAAC0lEQVR42mNk+A8AAQUBAScY42YAAAAASUVORK5CYII='
)
png_1px_b64 = base64.b64encode(png_1px)

# raw bytes in
b1 = ImageBlock(image=png_1px)
print('raw->', b1.image == png_1px_b64, b1.image_mimetype)

# already-base64 bytes in
b2 = ImageBlock(image=png_1px_b64)
print('b64->', b2.image == png_1px_b64, b2.image_mimetype)

print(normalize_image_bytes(png_1px) == (png_1px_b64, png_1px))
print(normalize_image_bytes(png_1px_b64) == (png_1px_b64, png_1px))
"

Already tested, all existing tests pass. Can you build get_image_mimetype() as a separate concern that composes with/calls normalize_image_bytes for the decode step, so we don't stomp each other? 46	class TextBlock(BaseModel):
47	    block_type: Literal["text"] = "text"
48	    text: str
49	
50	
51	def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
52	    """Normalize raw or base64-encoded image bytes.
53	
54	    Detects whether ``image`` is already base64-encoded by checking that it
55	    round-trips through decode/encode. Returns a tuple of
56	    ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where ``encoded_image`` is
57	    always base64-encoded bytes and ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the raw,
58	    decoded bytes suitable for mimetype guessing.
59	    """
60	    try:
61	        decoded = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
62	        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image:
63	            return image, decoded
64	    except Exception:
65	        pass
66	
67	    return base64.b64encode(image), image
68	
69	
70	class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
71	    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
72	    image: bytes | None = None
73	    path: FilePath | None = None
74	    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
75	    image_mimetype: str | None = None
76	    detail: str | None = None
77	
78	    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
79	    @classmethod
80	    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
81	        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
82	        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
83	            return url
84	        return AnyUrl(url=url)
85	
86	    @model_validator(mode="after")
87	    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
88	        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.
89	
90	        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
91	        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
92	        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
93	        """
94	        if not self.image:
95	            return self
96	
97	        encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
98	
99	        if not self.image_mimetype:
100	            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
101	            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
102	
103	        self.image = encoded_image
104	
105	        return self
106	
107	    def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
108	        """Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.
109	
110	        Args:
111	            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes
112	        """
113	        if self.image is not None:
114	            if as_base64:
115	                return BytesIO(self.image)
116	            return BytesIO(base64.b64decode(self.image))
117	        elif self.path is not None:
118	            img_bytes = self.path.read_bytes()
119	            if as_base64:
120	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
121	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
122	        elif self.url is not None:
123	            # load image from URL
124	            response = requests.get(str(self.url))
125	            img_bytes = response.content
126	            if as_base64:
127	                return BytesIO(base64.b64encode(img_bytes))
128	            return BytesIO(img_bytes)
129	        else:
130	            raise ValueError("No image found in the chat message!")
131	
132	
133	ContentBlock = Annotated[
134	    Union[TextBlock, ImageBlock], Field(discriminator="block_type")
135	]
136	
137	
138	class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
139	    """Chat message."""
140	
141	    role: MessageRole = MessageRole.USER
142	    additional_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
143	    blocks: list[ContentBlock] = Field(default_factory=list)
144	
145	    def __init__(self, /, content: Any | None = None, **data: Any) -> None:
146	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
147	
148	        If content was passed and contained text, store a single TextBlock.
149	        If content was passed and it was a list, assume it's a list of content blocks and store it.
150	        """
151	        if content is not None:
152	            if isinstance(content, str):
153	                data["blocks"] = [TextBlock(text=content)]
154	            elif isinstance(content, list):
155	                data["blocks"] = content
156	
157	        super().__init__(**data)
158	
159	    @property
160	    def content(self) -> str | None:
161	        """Keeps backward compatibility with the old `content` field.
162	
163	        Returns:
164	            The cumulative content of the blocks if they're all of type TextBlock, None otherwise.
165	        """
166	        content = ""
167	        for block in self.blocks:
168	            if not isinstance(block, TextBlock):
169	                return None
170	            content += block.text
171	
172	        return content
173	
174	    @content.setter
175	    def content(self, content: str) -> None:

index 00e30cf23..824a735f4 100644
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -48,6 +48,25 @@ class TextBlock(BaseModel):
     text: str
 
 
+def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
+    """Normalize raw or base64-encoded image bytes.
+
+    Detects whether ``image`` is already base64-encoded by checking that it
+    round-trips through decode/encode. Returns a tuple of
+    ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where ``encoded_image`` is
+    always base64-encoded bytes and ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the raw,
+    decoded bytes suitable for mimetype guessing.
+    """
+    try:
+        decoded = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
+        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image:
+            return image, decoded
+    except Exception:
+        pass
+
+    return base64.b64encode(image), image
+
+
 class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
     block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
     image: bytes | None = None
@@ -75,11 +94,13 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         if not self.image:
             return self
 
+        encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
+
         if not self.image_mimetype:
-            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
+            guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
             self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
 
-        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
+        self.image = encoded_image
 
         return self

content.text:
Patch written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Tests pass (12/12).

Summary: Added `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` in `types.py`, which detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (via a decode/re-encode round-trip check) and returns `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`. `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` now delegates to this helper instead of unconditionally re-encoding and guessing on raw bytes, so pre-encoded base64 images no longer get double-encoded and mimetype guessing always runs on decoded bytes. Verified manually with both raw and pre-base64-encoded PNG inputs. Coordinated with agent2, who is working on a `get_image_mimetype()` accessor in the same file but in an apparently separate sandbox — no actual file conflict observed on my end.

Summary: Added `normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]` in `types.py`, which detects whether input bytes are already base64-encoded (via a decode/re-encode round-trip check) and returns `(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`. `ImageBlock.image_to_base64` now delegates to this helper instead of unconditionally re-encoding and guessing on raw bytes, so pre-encoded base64 images no longer get double-encoded and mimetype guessing always runs on decoded bytes. Verified manually with both raw and pre-base64-encoded PNG inputs. Coordinated with agent2, who is working on a `get_image_mimetype()` accessor in the same file but in an apparently separate sandbox — no actual file conflict observed on my end.
